executive_summary: Your amber status is entirely driven by geopolitical concentration among suppliers across three high-risk zones: China (six suppliers spanning EMS, nicotine, batteries, and printing substrates), NATO's eastern flank (Stora Enso in Finland), and secondary hotspots (South Korea near North Korea, India with dual border tensions, South Africa with unexplained equity weakness). The immediate priority is your two critical-exposure EMS suppliers (Huizhou BYD and Smoore) plus EVE Energy on batteries—all three are China-exposed and vulnerable to US export controls on semiconductors and advanced materials, which directly threaten your electronic modular systems. Secondary but actionable: ITC's high nicotine exposure combined with India-China border risk could force tariffs or supply interruption within 6–12 months; you should map alternate nicotine sourcing now rather than reactively. Stora Enso and Sappi's equity decline warrant outreach calls (not urgent, but rule out demand destruction or working capital stress before Q2 earnings). Macro and peer pillars are stable, so this is a pure supplier geography and concentration problem—consider dual-sourcing your EMS and battery tiers toward non-China regions or onshoring, and consolidate your printing substrate base away from geopolitical frontlines.
